headline: Submit the implementation or qualification brief that matches your buying path
subheadline: This page is the lead-form-first close path for buyers who want help before they move into software, implementation, or enterprise rollout.
stepsHeadline: The contact funnel should create clarity before anyone follows up

steps.title: Submit the brief
steps.description: Describe the site, funnel, implementation, or rollout outcome you want help achieving.

steps.title: Route the opportunity
steps.description: The lead is recorded with source, offer interest, and follow-up stage so it can be worked inside the admin queue.

steps.title: Continue the right close
steps.description: Operator buyers can move into software, Launch Sprint buyers can be scoped, and enterprise buyers can be qualified properly.

sections.title: Best for self-serve buyers
sections.description: If you are ready to buy the software now, go back to pricing or the Operator OS offer and use the direct checkout path.

sections.title: Best for buyers who need implementation
sections.description: If you want help with positioning, pages, automations, or operational setup, use this form to begin the Launch Sprint conversation.

sections.title: Best for enterprise buyers
sections.description: If you need self-hosting, governance, or a customized rollout, use this form to start the qualification process before commercial scope is finalized.

leadForm.heading: Start the right follow-up path
leadForm.intro: Share enough context to help VoiceToWebsite route you into software checkout, implementation scope, or enterprise qualification without losing the trail inside the admin queue.

faq.question: Why use a lead form instead of forcing everyone into a calendar booking flow?
faq.answer: Because this pass is intentionally lead-form-first. It captures intent cleanly, routes the opportunity in the admin queue, and avoids pretending there is a scheduler dependency when there is not.
